what_it_does: This file is a terrain-entry ledger record that inventories an external ComfyUI data store at /home/tim/comfyui-data, describing its model-weights and saved-workflows contents, size, activity dates, and its provisional relation to the Company system.
observations: The entry describes a 36GB external data directory with only 36 files, split into models/ and workflows/.
It lists 13 saved JSON workflows themed around character generation, IPAdapter/FaceID, upscaling, and video.
The biggest weights include a 9.1GB T5 text encoder, a 6.5GB RealVisXL checkpoint, and a 5.9GB LTX-Video checkpoint.
grep for /home/tim/company references in JSON and Markdown returned zero hits.
It is not a git repository and has no git remote.
Coverage metadata says 0 of 36 files were read; only directory structure and listings were inspected.
standouts: A 36GB external ComfyUI weights/workflows store with no detected Company references.
Relation is explicitly 'candidate' based on absence of connection evidence.
Workflow themes focus on character image generation, identity transfer, and video.
Large binary weight files were not opened or inspected.
concerns: Coverage is 0/36 files read, so the entry relies on directory listings rather than content inspection.
Status is 'unconfirmed' and the Company link is inferred only from absence of evidence.
Large binary model weights are uninspected, leaving licensing and provenance unknown.
No git history or remote exists, so change tracking is absent.
notes: A candidate, unconfirmed external ComfyUI weights and workflows store (36GB) with no detected Company references and only structural coverage.
questions: Which specific ComfyUI installation or process consumes this data directory?
Are these workflows derived from or used by any Company project?
What are the licenses and provenance of the large model weights?
Why is the data stored outside the main ComfyUI repository?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to describe and assess the data store as a ledger terrain-entry, which matches what the file does.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to establish a factual boundary record for a large external image-generation asset store so the orienteering ledger can later decide whether it belongs to the Company system.
novelty: It treats a non-runnable, binary-heavy data directory as a ledger terrain-entry with coverage metadata and an explicit 'zero Company references' relation assessment.
contribution: Provides a structured, evidence-backed inventory of a large external asset store, including size, file categories, workflow themes, and a provisional relation status, which a fused system can use for provenance and inclusion decisions.
summary_for_embedding: Terrain ledger entry describing /home/tim/comfyui-data, a 36GB external ComfyUI model-weights and saved-workflows store with 13 workflows, no Company references, and candidate relation status.
intention_for_embedding: To document and assess whether a large external ComfyUI data directory belongs to the Company system.
